title: Unit 1 Test: Place Value of Whole Numbers

problems.question: In the number 58,342, what is the value of the digit 8?
problems.solution_steps: The 8 is in the thousands place, so its value is 8 x 1,000 = 8,000.
problems.hint: Count the places from the right: ones, tens, hundreds, thousands.

problems.question: The value of a digit in one place is ___ times the value of the same digit in the place to its right.
problems.solution_steps: Each place to the left is 10 times greater than the place to its right.
problems.hint: Think about how tens relate to ones.

problems.question: Which is the standard form of 'sixty-two thousand, four hundred five'?
problems.hint: There are no tens in 'four hundred five'.

problems.question: What is the word form of 304,027?
choices: A) three hundred four thousand, twenty-seven
B) three hundred forty thousand, twenty-seven
D) three hundred four thousand, two hundred seven
problems.hint: Read the thousands period first, then the hundreds period.

problems.question: In 770, the 7 in the hundreds place has a value 10 times greater than the 7 in the tens place.
problems.solution_steps: Hundreds place value = 700; tens place value = 70. 700 / 70 = 10.

problems.question: Which shows 73,506 in expanded form?
problems.hint: There are no tens, so skip that term.

problems.question: Which symbol makes the statement true? 45,219 ___ 45,291
problems.solution_steps: Ten-thousands, thousands, hundreds all match. Compare tens: 1 < 9, so 45,219 < 45,291.

problems.question: Which number is greatest?
problems.solution_steps: Compare ten-thousands: 9 > 8, so 98,234 is larger than any 8x,xxx number.

problems.question: Round 47,638 to the nearest thousand.
problems.solution_steps: Hundreds digit is 6 (>= 5), so round up: 47,638 -> 48,000.

problems.question: Round 152,490 to the nearest ten-thousand.
problems.solution_steps: Thousands digit is 2 (< 5), so round down: 152,490 -> 150,000.

problems.question: 83,450 is less than 83,405.
problems.solution_steps: Ten-thousands, thousands, hundreds match. Compare tens: 5 > 0, so 83,450 > 83,405.

problems.question: The 5 in 52,000 is how many times the value of the 5 in 5,200?
What number is represented by 400,000 + 20,000 + 700 + 60 + 3?
problems.hint: There are no thousands in the expanded form.

problems.question: A stadium has 68,549 seats. Rounded to the nearest thousand AND to the nearest ten-thousand, what are the two estimates?
problems.solution_steps: Nearest thousand: hundreds digit 5 -> round up to 69,000. Nearest ten-thousand: thousands digit 8 -> round up to 70,000.

problems.question: Order from least to greatest: 76,540; 76,450; 67,540; 76,504.
problems.hint: First separate the 67,xxx from the 76,xxx numbers.
